scenes.text:
The moment your ego walks
in, your leadership walks out.

Ego-driven leaders surround themselves with yes men. They take credit for wins and deflect blame for losses. They stop learning because they believe they
already know everything. And slowly, surely, they destroy the very thing they built. The best leaders are humble enough to learn, listen, and adapt.

Check your ego daily. Are you making decisions for the mission or for your pride? Are you listening to feedback or dismissing
it? The strongest leaders are the ones humble enough to say I do not know but I will figure it out.

Humility is not thinking less of yourself.
It is thinking of yourself less.

How do you keep your ego in check?
Share your method. Follow for humble leadership.
